channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Gene therapy is transforming lives, but for many Americans it's hard to reach
item.description: Gene therapies have the potential to cure some diseases, but they are extraordinarily expensive. Location can also be a big hurdle for patients seekin...

item.title: Will AI Solve Immunology’s Debate Over “Self vs. Non-Self?”
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE In 1872, English mathematician and sometimes poet, Augustus de Morgan, wrote this catching rhyme: “Great fleas have little fleas upon th...

item.title: 'Fear of Flying Clinic' helps anxious travelers back into the skies
item.description: For 50 years, a San Francisco-based group has created a space where fearful flyers can get supported exposure to air travel.

item.title: China and the US alter foreign aid strategies
item.description: China's foreign aid strategy has shifted in the last few decades and now its model may be the one the US is adopting as China moves away from it.

item.title: Hospitals Fighting Measles Confront a Challenge: Few Doctors Have Seen It Before
item.description: As the number of cases grows to about 1,000 in the Carolinas, health care workers who’ve never seen the vaccine-preventable disease can get caught by ...

item.title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
item.description: The "KFF Health News Minute” brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

item.title: BAFTAs apologize after guest with Tourette syndrome uses racial slur during ceremony
item.description: A man with Tourette syndrome shouted a racial slur and other offensive remarks during the BAFTA awards ceremony Sunday. The BBC did not edit out his o...

item.title: The FDA creates a quicker path for gene therapies
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration aims to evaluate treatments for rare diseases based on plausible evidence that they would work — without requiring a ...

item.title: As More Americans Embrace Anxiety Treatment, MAHA Derides Medications
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has criticized the broadening use of anxiety medications, claiming they’re harmful. Doctors and researchers say the MAHA movemen...

item.title: Más personas toman medicamentos para tratar la ansiedad, aunque el gobierno critica su uso
item.description: La proporción de adultos en el país que tomaron medicamentos para la ansiedad aumentó de 11,7% en 2019 a 14,3% en 2024, y la mayor parte del increment...

item.title: New Orleans Brings Back the House Call, Sending Nurses To Visit Newborns and Moms
item.description: Louisiana is one of the worst-performing states when it comes to health outcomes of mothers and infants. New Orleans is trying to catch health issues ...

item.title: Can We Ride the GenAI Wave Without Getting Subsumed by It?
item.description: By DAVID SHAYWITZ “There are decades where nothing happens; and there are weeks where decades happen,” said Lenin, probably never. It’s also a remarka...

item.title: Sofia Noori, Nema Health
item.description: Nema Health has cracked the code on PTSD and has an amazing study showing that their technique of intensive cognitive processing therapy is over 99% e...

item.title: AI replaces me: The Private AI Bubble Meets the Public Digital Health Reality Check
item.description: By CLAUDE.AI trying to write like me MATTHEW HOLT I finally did what I have been wanting to do for a while and had an AI ingest everything I’ve writte...

item.title: Amodei Believes “AI-Enabled Autocracy” Could Be Imminent
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE Last month, Dario Amodei, CEO of Anthropic, did it again – scaring the bejesus out of societal leaders worldwide with warnings that thei...
